moat.description: QuickBooks dominates as a system of record for 7M+ SMBs, but agentic AI startups (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) and IRS Direct File expansion have mounted credible threats to the bundle's premium.
moat.analysisSummary: Intuit's moat is **revenue-weighted**: Global Business Solutions (QuickBooks) at ~58% of FY2026's $21.1B guided revenue remains the core fortress; TurboTax (~24%), Credit Karma (~12%), and ProTax (~3%) are supporting pillars with varying AI vulnerability.

analysisPoints.title: QuickBooks: The Unassailable System of Record (58%)
analysisPoints.text: 7M+ SMBs use QuickBooks as their system of record for payroll, invoicing, taxes, and lending. Years of embedded transaction history make migration nearly impossible — the 24% growth in QBO Online Accounting in Q2 FY2026 and 18% Global Business Solutions segment growth confirm continued pricing power and deepening lock-in.

analysisPoints.title: TurboTax: Weakening but Managed (24%)
analysisPoints.text: IRS Direct File expanded to 25 states covering 30M+ eligible taxpayers; Intuit exited the IRS Free File Program; and the Direct File Act of 2026 debates a federal mandate for free filing. TurboTax Live (human-assisted) remains the pivot to higher-ARPU defensible territory, guided +8% in FY2026, but the secular headwind is structural.

analysisPoints.title: AI-Native Challenger Risk to the Bundle
analysisPoints.text: Startups Puzzle, Digits, and Campfire are deploying agentic AI to automate up to 98% of bookkeeping tasks. Anthropic's Managed Agents launch sparked a sector-wide re-rating of bundled SaaS businesses. Intuit's bundling moat has shifted from 'unassailable all-in-one platform' to 'best-but-contested platform' — the moat still holds but the valuation premium has been structurally cut.

growth.description: Q2 FY2026 revenue of $4.65B (+17% YoY) beat the $4.53B consensus; QuickBooks Online Accounting grew +24% and Global Business Solutions +18%. FY2026 full-year guidance reiterated at $20.997–21.186B (+12–13%), with GAAP EPS of $15.49–15.69 (+13–15%) and Non-GAAP EPS of $22.98–23.18. Growth has decelerated from the 15–20% band into the 10–14% range as TurboTax faces IRS Direct File pressure and AI-native bookkeeping tools mount a credible challenge to the bundle. Score downgraded from 84: CAGR profile has dropped from the 15–30% band to the 8–15% band — a structural shift, not a cyclical blip.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 67 (8–15% CAGR) + 5 recurring subscription (QuickBooks high switching costs, ~65% subscription mix) + 3 AI TAM expansion (Intuit Assist Deduction Maximizer, QBO AI agentic features in beta) − 2 competitive headwinds (AI-native startups + IRS Direct File Act debate) = 73

drivers.name: Credit Karma
drivers.metric: +10–13% FY2026 guided; consumer credit recovery tailwind

drivers.name: Consumer (TurboTax)
drivers.metric: +8% FY2026 guided; TurboTax Live was +47% in FY2025

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If the Direct File Act of 2026 passes into federal law by end of FY2026, expanding IRS free filing to all income levels nationally, TurboTax unit volumes could fall 15–20% and the Consumer segment misses its FY2027 growth trajectory — removing ~$1.5B of high-margin Consumer revenue from the model

valuation.description: Trading ~$355 — 17% below the base case ($430) — as AI disruption fears have compressed the forward GAAP P/E from ~45× to ~23×. At ~16× non-GAAP forward earnings and ~4.7× NTM sales, valuation is the most attractive since 2019. The selloff reflects real structural risks but has overshot fundamentals given the Q2 FY2026 beat and reiterated full-year guidance.

peAnalysis.summary: At ~23× GAAP forward P/E, Intuit trades below the quality-software sector median (~28–32×) and at its lowest multiple in 6+ years — a significant discount for a business generating $6.1B in annual FCF with 7M+ switching-cost SMB customers. On a non-GAAP basis the forward P/E drops to ~15× and the PEG to ~1.1×, deep into growth-at-a-reasonable-price territory. The narrow trailing-to-forward P/E gap (26× → 23×) reflects an earnings ramp from operating leverage, not deceleration — the AI fear compression has overshot fundamentals.

bear.description: Direct File Act passes federally, AI-native bookkeeping achieves mass SMB adoption, and a tariff-driven recession spikes QuickBooks churn.
points: Direct File Act of 2026 passes into federal law, expanding IRS free filing to all income levels and removing ~30%+ of TurboTax's addressable market by FY2028
AI-native bookkeeping startups (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) capture 10%+ of the QuickBooks SMB segment by end of FY2027, compressing ARPU below $1,500/customer
Tariff-driven SMB failures in manufacturing and retail spike QuickBooks annualised churn above 8%, reversing the Global Business Solutions growth trajectory

base.description: FY2026 guidance achieved, QuickBooks pricing power holds, AI-native challengers remain subscale through 2027, and multiple partially re-rates.
points: Global Business Solutions sustains 14–15% growth driven by QBO ARPU expansion above $2,000/customer and mid-market QuickBooks adoption
Intuit Assist Deduction Maximizer achieves meaningful SMB paid penetration, adding $200M+ incremental revenue by FY2027
Operating margins expand 100–150 bps as AI reduces support headcount and fixed cost structure, with GAAP EPS reaching $18+ in FY2027

bull.description: QuickBooks becomes the financial operating system for 12M+ SMBs with AI-driven monetisation inflecting materially and Credit Karma re-accelerating.
points: International QuickBooks reaches critical mass in UK, Canada, and ANZ, contributing 20%+ of segment growth by FY2028 and adding $1B+ incremental revenue
B2B payments volume through QuickBooks doubles to $200B+ annually, adding a high-margin transaction-embedded revenue layer that re-rates the platform multiple
Credit Karma's lending platform inflects as the rate cycle normalises, growing 25%+ in FY2027 and becoming a $3B+ annual revenue segment

learnedInterfaces.note: TurboTax's guided interview format is directly challenged by agentic AI (Anthropic Managed Agents) that offer conversational, zero-UI tax filing — abstracting away the learned workflow.

businessLogic.note: AI-native startups (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) claim to automate 98% of bookkeeping; IRS Direct File is a zero-cost government competitor for simple returns. Intuit's simplification logic is no longer uniquely defensible.

publicDataAccess.note: Tax forms and IRS regulations are public — AI navigates them efficiently, commoditising Intuit's simplification value. The real moat is proprietary data, not public-data navigation.

talentScarcity.note: AI has democratised access to tax and accounting expertise, lowering the barrier that previously made TurboTax's guided logic and CPA-trained models difficult to replicate.

bundling.note: The TurboTax + QuickBooks + Credit Karma + Mailchimp suite remains uniquely integrated, but the shift to modular agentic AI tools (API-first architectures from Puzzle, Digits) is eroding the all-in-one premium — downgraded from strong to intact this quarter.

proprietaryData.note: Tax return data, SMB financial records, and credit profiles for 100M+ users is an irreplaceable dataset that improves underwriting, personalization, and AI model training. AI makes this moat stronger, not weaker.

regulatoryLockIn.note: IRS authorized e-file partner status, state tax agency relationships, payroll tax compliance, and financial institution integrations remain intact. The Direct File Act of 2026 is a monitored legislative risk — not yet passed.

networkEffects.note: CPA-client ecosystem: accountants recommend QuickBooks → clients adopt → data improves → product gets stickier. The 7M+ SMB network is self-reinforcing with high cross-product adoption.

transactionEmbedding.note: Payroll runs, invoicing, tax filing, expense tracking, and lending are all embedded in the daily operations of 7M+ small businesses — removing Intuit requires re-engineering core business processes.

systemOfRecord.note: QuickBooks is the system of record for 7M+ small business finances; TurboTax holds the authoritative tax history of tens of millions of households. Migration risk is prohibitive.

tenMoats.verdict: Intuit is a net beneficiary of AI on balance — its proprietary financial dataset (100M+ users' tax returns, SMB financials, credit profiles) becomes more valuable as the AI training-data arms race intensifies, and the transactionEmbedding and systemOfRecord moats are structurally immune to LLM substitution. The chief AI risk is bundling: agentic point-solutions (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) can replicate individual features cheaply, eroding the premium for the all-in-one suite. The 2-point gap between moatScore (87) and aiResilienceScore (85) reflects that Anthropic Managed Agents directly target Intuit's core tax and accounting workflows — a specific, named AI threat already in market. In the AI era, Intuit's durability depends on whether its data flywheel monetisation (Intuit Assist, Deduction Maximizer) accelerates faster than challenger bundling erodes.
